title: Politics and government
explanation:
Politics and government

  • election, parliament, policy, candidate, coalition
  • legislation, referendum, opposition, campaign, mandate
  • verbs: vote, govern, debate, legislate

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Citizens will vote in the general ____ next month. | *election | platform | receipt
The new ____ aims to reduce carbon emissions by 2030. | *policy | slogan | habit
Several parties formed a ____ to stay in power. | *coalition | subscription | runway
The ____ debated the proposed law for hours. | *parliament | laboratory | terminal

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: Each ____ presented their programme to voters. | candidate
The government introduced new ____ on data protection. | legislation
The ____ called for an investigation into the scandal. | opposition
Activists organised a ____ to raise public support. | campaign
The president claimed a strong ____ after winning decisively. | mandate

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The country held a ____ on whether to leave the trade bloc. | *referendum | deadline | discount
Politicians often ____ complex issues in televised discussions. | *debate | repair | depart
Elected officials are expected to ____ responsibly. | *govern | commute | subscribe
Every citizen over eighteen has the right to ____. | *vote | charge | queue
